factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in residential and commercial water treatment.

evidence.text: During 2018 and 2017, we continued execution of certain business restructuring initiatives aimed at reducing our fixed cost structure and realigned our business in contemplation of the Separation and Distribution of nVent. We expect these actions will contribute to margin growth in 2019.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key markets, indicating positive momentum.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

evidence.text: We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices to mitigate inflation, a positive sign for margins.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by core sales increases across all segments.

evidence.text: The 4.2 percent increase in consolidated net sales in 2018 from 2017 was primarily the result of: •core sales increases across all three reportable segments, primarily driven by increased sales in the residential and commercial businesses;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating organic revenue growth across segments, a clear positive trend.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the residential and commercial businesses.

evidence.text: The 9.2 percent increase in net sales for Aquatic Systems in 2018 from 2017 was primarily the result of: •core sales growth related to higher sales of certain pool products primarily serving North American residential housing;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing unit volumes, particularly in residential and commercial sectors, indicating positive demand.

factors.summary: End markets such as residential and commercial water treatment are growing fastest.

evidence.text: We are reinforcing our businesses to more effectively address these opportunities through research and development and additional sales and marketing resources. Unless we successfully penetrate these markets, our core sales growth will likely be limited or may decline.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals as end markets are growing but success depends on market penetration.

factors.summary: Raw material and component costs are rising, and the company is offsetting them through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: We have experienced material and other cost inflation. We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation. We expect the current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Costs are rising but being offset by pricing and productivity, a balanced situation.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to pricing increases and cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: The 0.6 percentage point increase in gross profit as a percentage of sales in 2018 from 2017 was primarily the result of: •selective increases in selling prices across all three reportable segments to mitigate inflationary cost increases;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are expanding due to pricing and cost initiatives, a clear positive development.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: The 0.8 percentage point decrease in SG&A expense as a percentage of sales in 2018 from 2017 and was driven by: •savings generated from restructuring and other lean initiatives;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, a strong positive outcome.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through lean initiatives and supply chain management.

evidence.text: The 0.1 point increase in segment income for Flow Technologies as a percentage of net sales in 2018 from 2017 was primarily the result of: •higher core sales in our commercial and specialty businesses, which resulted in increased leverage on fixed operating expenses;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in operational efficiency through lean initiatives and supply chain management indicates a positive trend.

factors.summary: Proposed regulations may impact the company's financial condition and tax rate.

evidence.text: Proposed regulations as part of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, enacted in the U.S. in December 2017, may place limitations on the deductibility of certain interest expense for U.S. tax purposes. These proposed regulations could materially adversely affect our financial condition, results of operations, cash flows or our effective tax rate in future reporting periods when enacted.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Proposed regulations could materially adversely affect financial condition and tax rate, posing a significant risk.

factors.summary: Management is confident in growth opportunities, as evidenced by investments in new products and markets.

evidence.text: In 2019, our operating objectives include the following: •Delivering our growth priorities through new products and global and market expansion, specifically in the areas of pool and residential and commercial water treatment especially through acquisitions and focus on China and Southeast Asia;
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence in growth opportunities is evident through investments in new products and markets.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include cost inflation and potential challenges in penetrating new markets.

evidence.text: We have experienced material and other cost inflation. We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation. We expect the current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include cost inflation and challenges in new market penetration, indicating moderate headwinds.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on acquisitions, product innovation, and market expansion.

evidence.text: In January 2019, as part of Filtration Solutions, we entered into definitive agreements to acquire Aquion and Pelican for $160.0 million and $120.0 million in cash, respectively, and subject to certain customary adjustments. We completed the Aquion acquisition on February 13, 2019 and the Pelican acquisition on February 12, 2019.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on acquisitions, product innovation, and market expansion suggests a strong long-term positive strategy.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange rates and global economic conditions.

evidence.text: The 4.2 percent increase in consolidated net sales in 2018 from 2017 was primarily the result of: •favorable foreign currency effects during the year ended December 31, 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange rates and global economic conditions are key sensitivities, but evidence shows favorable foreign currency effects.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches and successful acquisitions.

evidence.text: In January 2019, as part of Filtration Solutions, we entered into definitive agreements to acquire Aquion and Pelican for $160.0 million and $120.0 million in cash, respectively, and subject to certain customary adjustments. We completed the Aquion acquisition on February 13, 2019 and the Pelican acquisition on February 12, 2019.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and successful acquisitions, such as the Aquion and Pelican acquisitions, indicate positive catalysts.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging through lower debt levels and early extinguishment of debt.

evidence.text: The 62.7 percent decrease in net interest expense in 2018 from 2017 was primarily the result of: •the impact of lower debt levels during 2018 compared to 2017. In June 2018, the proceeds from the Separation were utilized to repay the remaining $255.3 million aggregate principal amount of our 2.9% fixed rate senior notes due 2018 and for the early extinguishment of €363.4 million aggregate principal amount of our 2.45% senior notes due 2019.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant deleveraging through lower debt levels and early extinguishment of debt shows a very positive development.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value through market expansion and product diversification.

evidence.text: In January 2019, as part of Filtration Solutions, we entered into definitive agreements to acquire Aquion and Pelican for $160.0 million and $120.0 million in cash, respectively, and subject to certain customary adjustments. We completed the Aquion acquisition on February 13, 2019 and the Pelican acquisition on February 12, 2019.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions are creating value through market expansion and product diversification.

factors.summary: Overall economic conditions are a tailwind, with growth in end markets such as housing and water treatment.

evidence.text: The following trends and uncertainties affected our financial performance in 2018 and 2017, and will likely impact our results in the future: •During 2018 and 2017, we continued execution of certain business restructuring initiatives aimed at reducing our fixed cost structure and realigned our business in contemplation of the Separation and Distribution of nVent. We expect these actions will contribute to margin growth in 2019.
evidence.subsection: Key trends and uncertainties regarding our existing business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Overall economic conditions are a tailwind with growth in end markets such as housing and water treatment.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are favorably impacting net sales.

evidence.text: The 4.2 percent increase in consolidated net sales in 2018 from 2017 was primarily the result of: •favorable foreign currency effects during the year ended December 31, 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are favorably impacting net sales.
